properties.name: Alta California (province-wide counts)
properties.summary: The whole colony, counted. In 1790 the entire Spanish population of Alta California was 910 people, inside a mission population of 7,353. Duran's 1833 ledger then states the mission system's demographic arithmetic in three numbers: 82,882 baptized since founding, 58,417 dead, 10,482 living.
series: 8,323 souls total: 910 gente de razon + 7,353 Indios (per-establishment table)
mission ledger: 64,675 baptized / 41,767 died / 20,238 living since founding
'under 25,000, mostly Indians'; ~4,000 gente de razon, ~1,800 able to bear arms
Duran's cumulative Cuadro Estadistico: 82,882 baptized / 58,417 died / 10,482 living
properties.result: One province, three generations, counted into decline

sources.citation: All rows from the Archives of California survey catalogs; C-A 50 unless noted. Figures are the documents' own.

properties.notes: Placed at Monterey as the capital; the counts are province-wide.

properties.name: Antigua (Baja) California (province-wide)
properties.summary: The old province counted alongside the new: Arrillaga's 1791 census tables cover 18 poblaciones, presidio and missions, with the men, women, and children of each.
series: per-mission families/souls table, ~17 missions (Loreto mission 76 souls…)
province-wide censo y castas table, 18 poblaciones

sources.citation: All rows from the Archives of California survey catalogs; C-A 50 unless noted. Figures are the documents' own.

properties.notes: Placed at Loreto; see the individual Baja pins for 1790 figures.

properties.name: Pueblo San José de Guadalupe
properties.summary: The first pueblo, counted at 78 souls in 1790 — one europeo, 33 espanoles, 8 indios, 8 mulatos, 28 otras castas. Four decades later it had grown to 602.
series: 78 souls (1 europeo, 33 espanoles, 8 indios, 8 mulatos, 28 otras castas)
602 inhabitants: 431 gente de razon + 171 indigenas avecindados

sources.citation: All rows from the Archives of California survey catalogs; C-A 50 unless noted. Figures are the documents' own.

properties.name: Pueblo de Los Ángeles
properties.summary: The pueblo's whole arc in five counts: 139 souls in the 1790 province table, 148 in 1792, 274 in 1817, then the Mexican-era growth to over a thousand — with the padrones counting Indians separately, in the pueblo and on its ranchos.
series: 139 souls (province table); estado of population + property, named pobladores
148 souls (2 europeos, 57 espanoles, 17 mestizos, 15 indios, 57 mulatos)
274 souls in the pueblo, 537 in the jurisdiction ('no especifica los indios')

sources.citation: All rows from the Archives of California survey catalogs; C-A 50 unless noted. Figures are the documents' own.

properties.notes: The 1828 vs 1830 drop reflects changing padron scope as much as population; both are shown as recorded.

properties.name: Presidio of San Francisco
properties.summary: The 1790 padron names every household of the company with casta, birthplace, age, wife, and children — 144 souls at the Golden Gate. By 1830 the whole SF jurisdiction, missions included, counted 5,469, of whom 5,240 were Indians.
series: 144 souls (1 europeo, 78 espanoles, 16 indios, 5 mulatos, 44 castas); nominal padron

sources.citation: All rows from the Archives of California survey catalogs; C-A 50 unless noted. Figures are the documents' own.

properties.name: Presidio of Santa Bárbara
properties.summary: The 1785 padron is a nominal roll of 67 heads of household — officers, sergeants, corporals, soldiers, vecinos, and the San Blas paquebot's servants — with casta, age, and family.
series: 67 heads of household, nominal roll with casta and family

sources.citation: All rows from the Archives of California survey catalogs; C-A 50 unless noted. Figures are the documents' own.

properties.name: Presidio of Loreto (Baja)
properties.summary: The old capital counted in 1790: 399 souls, a fifth of them under the fuero militar.
series: 399 souls (3 europeos, 73 espanoles, 152 indios, 10 mulatos, 158 otras castas)

sources.citation: All rows from the Archives of California survey catalogs; C-A 50 unless noted. Figures are the documents' own.

properties.name: Real de Santa Ana (Baja mining district)
properties.summary: The southern mining district, the most casta-diverse place in either California: 696 souls with 5 miners, 16 rancheros, and 93 others by trade.
series: 696 souls (3 europeos, 133 espanoles, 178 indios, 157 mulatos, 204 castas)

sources.citation: All rows from the Archives of California survey catalogs; C-A 50 unless noted. Figures are the documents' own.

properties.name: Mission San Carlos (Carmel)
properties.summary: Neophyte population as the annual registros and the 1790 censo campaign recorded it.

sources.citation: All rows from the Archives of California survey catalogs; C-A 50 unless noted. Figures are the documents' own.

properties.notes: The counts are of people living AT the mission when counted; flight, death, and new baptism churn beneath every figure. A 1795 nominal padron of the Monterey-district missions exists (C-A 50 Doc 54).
